sha.name: tenyuu grand
sha.detail:
Located in the heart of prime business area, TENYUU Grand is the one and only premium authentic Japanese restaurant that is surrounded by exquisite Japanese garden and large parking space. Embellished with element of wood and Sakura trees as interior highlights, we offer stunning Japanese sceneries and a welcoming atmosphere with attentive staffs and first class Japanese cuisine. Our wide range of finest and freshest ingredients are imported up to seven times a week from Japan and around the World, thus confirming our slogan as “Sushi Heaven” for every Japanese foods lover as we serve only the best and delectable creations from our top professional chefs. 
Our name “TENYUU” means “wealth from heaven”. It is reflected that we are an expert in Japanese cuisine with rich experiences in creating the best Japanese foods that will make you feel like tasting superb menus from heaven. We also provide 10 different V.I.P rooms both Japanese style and conventional style for special events, corporate dinners, birthday parties, or private dining experience. Great selection of sakes and wide range of alcoholic beverages are also available.
We open for lunch from 10.00 to 14.30, and from 17.30 to midnight for dinner. For reservation : 02-632-1777

sha.name: Sra Bua by Kiin Kiin
sha.detail:
Sra Bua by Kiin Kiin is widely recognised as ‘modern gastronomy at its best’ – making it very different from home-style cooking. Diners may experience a curry served with the texture and temperature of ice-cream and bites of lobster locked inside gelatinous pearls that melt in the mouth with a sip of Tom Yam broth. Everything from fish sauce to prawn crackers is made in the Sra Bua by Kiin Kiin kitchen.
Head Chef Chayawee invites guests to enjoy an extraordinary culinary performance as he choreographs, with his team, an amazing array of basic Thai ingredients taken from original recipes to create modern interpretations in daring combinations. The familiar flavours of traditional Thai dishes are well maintained in every exquisitely composed food presentation, prepared using global cooking techniques.
Guests are asked to dress elegant when dining at Sra Bua by Kiin Kiin. Gentlemen are required to wear long trousers and closed shoes. Please do not wear sports or athletic garments, slippers and sleeveless T-shirts.
The restaurant is suitable for children aged 6 years and above. We ask parents to ensure that their children are dressed appropriately

omtum Der, a collaboration of friends and family who have close tie to Isan region, caters authentic northern Isan food in a comfortable friendly atmosphere, which guarantees to offer more than tasty true Isan food but also a lifestyle that can be enjoyed.

sha.name: Siao Siew Restaurant
sha.detail:
The legend of "Siao Siew Restaurant, Nakhon Ratchasima"
“Chinese restaurant that has been in Korat for over 90 years”
If you are from Korat, you cannot deny that I don't know Xiao Siew Restaurant. This restaurant is not only well known to the people of Korat. Even people from foreign countries, including foreigners, frequented by the service. Everyone who comes to use the service is impressed with the service and the taste of the food. But did you know that before becoming the Xiao Xiao restaurant known today? The founder of this restaurant had to use his patience and perseverance to build up his reputation and gain experience……to get to this day.
Mr. Siheang Saelim, founder of Xiao Xiao restaurant, born in 1908, is a Chinese man who, like many Chinese people, migrated from poverty to China in search of a better future in Thailand. The life of Mr. Sui Heang before coming to Thailand Since birth, he was orphaned by his father, with only a mother to provide for the family which consisted of two sons and one daughter. Each day that he goes out to work, he has to bring his three children with him. When Mr. Sui Heang is 16 years old, he decides to come to Thailand with his brother. The mother and older sister still live in Shantou Sub-district, Teng Hai District, People's Republic of China. Mr. Sui Heang and his brother, when they arrived in Thailand, found a job with his brother working as an employee in Bangkok. As for Mr. Sui Heang, he settled in Korat where he worked as an employee at a Kee Seng restaurant. Receive a salary of 7 baht per month. Which the money he earned, Mr. Zi Heang divided it back to his mother in China on a monthly basis. in which Mr. Sui Hieng's stewed duck, soft duck meat and rich stew resulting in a large number of regular customers When there was enough money saved from selling duck stew, Mr. Ziheang opened a porridge shop. Located in front of Thao Suranaree Monument It is only one shophouse, which Xin Sae named "Tia Zhu Sua" has named it. Which the money he earned, Mr. Zi Heang divided it back to his mother in China on a monthly basis. in which Mr. Sui Hieng's stewed duck, soft duck meat and rich stew resulting in a large number of regular customers When there was enough money saved from selling duck stew, Mr. Ziheang opened a porridge shop. Located in front of Thao Suranaree Monument It is only one shophouse, which Xin Sae named "Tia Zhu Sua" has named it. "Crescent"which means "lek lek", open for sale 24 hours a day, with 2 shifts of staff waiting to be a hand in hand and has invited his brother to help work at the shop. At that time, Mr. Siheang was 22 years old and married Ms. Yusiam Sae Chi, having 4 sons and 5 daughters. During World War II in 1940, Mr. Sui Heang and his family fled the war to Bua Yai District, Nakhon Ratchasima Province and opened a restaurant there at Bua Yai Railway Station. When World War II ended, Mr. Suiheang and his family moved back to Muang District, Nakhon Ratchasima Province to open a restaurant under the Pokphand Hotel (in the past) at Chom Phon Road. After about 4 years, the business expanded by moving. Opened a new shop behind Thao Suranaree Monument, Chumphon Road. Rented a 4 booth building with enough space to host a party. With perseverance, the business continued to progress. And on November 23, 1969 bought the land and built a 4-storey 4-storey building on Atsadang Road. And during this time, the eldest son, Mr. Iam Jai Samran and his wife Mrs. Somporn Jaisamran came to help run the business. make business progress rapidly Therefore, the idea was to expand it to meet the increasing number of customers, and on January 28, 1977 opened Xiao Siew Restaurant on Bua Rong Road, on an area of ????2 rai, where it is now located. The style of the restaurant was different from other restaurants in those days. Because it is designed to have a parking space under the building and a meeting room. Banquet room that can accommodate up to 1,500 people and has been constantly improving the style of food and service, making it popular with both residents of Nakhon Ratchasima and tourists, both Thai and foreigners. Currently, it is managed by Mr. Somporn Jaisamran (2nd generation heir) and Dr. Anat Chaisamran (3rd generation heir).
In his life, Mr. Sui Heang holds that “Where is the effort? Success is there.” Mr. Ziheang has taught all his children to be diligent, patient, honest. and act for the benefit of society which all children have adhered to the teachings of Mr. Sui Heang throughout Passed away peacefully at the age of 77, throughout the time Mr. Siheang lived in Thailand. In addition to conducting a profession in good faith also helps the society by participating in activities with various clubs and associations in Nakhon Ratchasima province regularly.

sha.name: Panya Indra Golf Course
sha.detail:
Panya Indra Golf Club
Premium Clean and Safety Golf destination in the Center of Bangkok
Panya Indra Golf Club consists of three nine-hole golf courses that have been designed and decorated in various styles. There are three types of courses: lakeside, palm, and botanical garden. Each of the golf courses was constructed and decorated in a unique style, and each was set at a different difficulty level. These three different types of golf courses are all located in a tropical setting, making it feel like you're playing golf in paradise.
The Panya Indra Golf Club is a famous destination for Thai and international golfers. The Golf Club is open for service six days a week on Tuesday through Sunday, with night service available on Tuesday through Friday.
Facilities
- Golf Driving Range: The driving range has an exceptional, high-quality design on real grass that will assist you in improving your swing and practicing your stroke.
- Panya Kitchen Restaurant: Panorama view space located on the Clubhouse's second floor. Serving international cuisine and beverages with original recipe since 1989.

sha.name: Nonna Nella by Lenzi
sha.detail: Nonna Nella by Lenzi comes from the love of the family and Italian food and traditions. Nonna Nella – which means Grandma Nella – is Chef Francesco Lenzi’s grandmother, born in Mistretta – Messina province- in Sicily in 1924 – a beautiful island located in South of Italy – and moved to Tuscany after adolescence. Nonna Nella loved to cook for her family and for Francesco’s friends and she transferred this passion to Chef Lenzi who knew how to make it an art by first opening the Lenzi Tuscan Kitchen, a well-known Italian fine dining restaurant in Bangkok. Chef Lenzi always had a dream, being able to cook for his customers just like his grandmother cooked for him: simply, authentic and with genuine products. Here the memory of the grandmother’s pizza scent has given life to a new concept in Bangkok where cooking and gastronomy are the master. Visit Nonna Nella’s laboratory !

sha.name: Canvas Restaurant
sha.detail:
Canvas serves a tasting menu highlighting Thai ingredients with focused cooking and vibrant flavors in a relaxed, open space.
 
Our menu changes seasonally to reflect the best local ingredients at each time of the year. We work closely with farmers and suppliers to source ingredients of top quality from all regions of Thailand, many of which are organically grown, others are wild, others carefully selected from local markets each morning.

We don't fit into a category of Thai or Western, but we’re influenced by many international cuisines, local street food and markets, traditional Thai preparations, modern techniques, and the city of Bangkok itself. The incredible abundance of Thai ingredients drives our cooking and we share that with our guests in a creative way. Our goal is to offer a unique dining experience that could only be had in Bangkok: bold, colorful, stimulating, and progressive.

data.name: Wat Triyang Wanaram (Wat Yang Sam Ton)
Wat Bot
data.introduction: Wat Bot was built in Ayutthaya period. Later, the temple was renovated on April 11, 1882. The old Ubosot or ordination hall of the Ayuthaya period, which has been respected by the local villagers and nearby residents, has a traditional style called "Mahaut"(without openings in all the side walls, except one door). However, the renovation work made it different from the original style, including its door arch and window frames, and only a front door and two windows on each side can be evidenced of the Ayutthaya style of art. The ordination hall is surrounded by niches  of double sandstone boundary stones in the Chinese style of art. One interesting is located behind the ordination hall. Since it was not restored, it is on dilapidated that it is impossible to structure, it can be noted that  this stupa is similar to those built in general in the Ayutthaya period, having a square relic chamber with 16-indented corners, and protruding niches in all four directions. The eastern side was made into a large chamber, but it was secretly dug and left in a damaged condition. Moreover, plenty pieces of red and grey sandstone Buddha images having lap widths of 0.30-1 metre were found.Considering the art appearing on the images, it was dated back to the 15-17 centuries

sha.name: No.43 Italian Bistro
sha.detail: Cape House is proud to play host to one of Bangkok’s premier Italian restaurants, the No.43 Italian Bistro. This cozy yet elegant restaurant offers the finest in Italian and Thai cuisine, prepared skillfully by Italian Chefs. It's authentic, delicious and reasonably priced dishes have seen No.43 become a popular restaurant for lunchtime business crowds and for evening diners who want an intimate and relaxed dining experience with excellent food.Opened in 1998, No.43 was designed to give its guests the feel of a true Italian bistro with its split-level, balcony and stalls configuration, white wood floors, black on white walls and cream tiled tabletops. Using the finest authentic ingredients, "No.43" offers many delicious dishes including outstanding pizzas, authentic Italian main courses, home made pastas, cakes and ice cream.Just five minutes' walk down Soi Langsuan from Chidlom BTS station, the restaurant is located on the ground floor of Cape House.In line with Cape&Kantary Hotels commitment to support the prevention of cruelty to animals, we do not serve Shark Fin, Bird’s Nest and Foie Gras in our restaurants.

sha.name: Baan PomPhet
sha.detail: Baan Pomphet is located in the historic City of Ayutthaya, the capital of the Kingdom of old Siam. Our site reveals the scenic views of Pomphet Fortress, an ancient ruin, and the iconic area of the Chao Phraya River and the Pasak River convergence. Many elements; 6 sizes of bircks were made from handcraft from the traditional way by firewood in brick oven. Metal and wood were perfectly combined, wooden lamp, fish-catching utensil name Chanang neatly made from ranttan; all were made from Thai craftsmen from many part of Thailand. Bodhi tree, the significant tree of Baan Pomphet reflecting unique sound of wind blowing. The nature from Baan Pomphet makes we feel relax. An outdoor pool with a wooden deck decorated with Thai modern architecture featuring the riverside restaurant that specializes in Charcoal Grilled River Prawns and traditional Thai cuisine. Our guests will experience the memorable golden hour at our rooftop bar with a variety of refreshing beverages and cocktails. We warmly welcome all our guests to explore an unseen viewpoint and local living scenery. Experience the moment at Baan Pomphet Ayutthaya.

sha.name: X2 Vibe Buriram Hotel
sha.detail: "X2 Vibe Buriram Hotel is a design hotel located in the eastern Thai city of Buriram. It is the first X2 Vibe design hotel in the region.The 5-story X2 Vibe Buriram hotel features 68 uniquely designed suites, in 3 different types. With a clear focus on design, that goes beyond that of other modern lifestyle hotels, and with an emphasis on the highest standard of service, this hotel will offer an experience unlike any other hotels in Buriram. Apart from all the facilities one would expect from a luxury hotel, fully equipped meeting facilities are available, and the 4K Caf? provides a range of drinks and refreshments. ViBar, an impressive rooftop bar, offers an extensive cocktail menu and wide views of the city.Buriram, which translates to “City of Happiness”, is known for its production of high quality pure silk, as well as for the popular Phanom Rung Historical Park. The park rests on an extinct volcano, and features a carefully restored Khmer temple as its stunning masterpiece.The X2 Vibe Buriram hotel is ideally located next to a shopping mall and supermarkets, and much to the delight of sports fans, in the vicinity of the famous Buriram United stadium and speedway. With 32,600 seats, the stadium is the largest club-owned football stadium in Thailand, and is nicknamed “Thunder Castle”. The Buriram United International Circuit speedway, located nearby, is suitable for Formula One racing, and even hosted a Japanese Super GT."

data.name: Sor Arunyik
data.introduction: The arunyik knife is a wisdom inherited from the people of Vientiane, Lao PDR. who migrated to Ban Ton Pho and Ban Phai Nong in Siam during the early Rattanakosin Era. From the past to the present, the villagers of Ban Ton Pho and Ban Phai Nong have conducted knife making as their main occupation and brought the products to be sold at Ban Arunyik because it was an important port. Once the buyers found the Arunyik knives were of good quality, they told others people that if you wanted to buy a quality knife, buy it at Ban Arunyik. So, this is the origin of the term "Arunyik knives."

Much like it did 19 years ago in its inception, it sets itself apart from the rest, with its infusion of youthful energy, love of music, passion for life, a touch of irreverence, and delivered with a deep belief in its credo of Love All, Serve All. It features 323 rooms, including exceptional rock-star suites, new accommodation for families with kids, luxurious digs for composers and executives. Dining in Pattaya took a rock notch higher with our restaurants-bars-lounges serving Pan Asian cuisine next to Mediterranean fare in a variety of stylish settings as well as renowned global Hard Rock Cafe. There are also Roxity Kids Club for kids, Tabu Club for families, Rock Spa and Rock Shops, and a list of live gigs and happenings to check-in and rock-out. We are committed to serve the highest standard of service to all our “Stars” and we as the “Band Members” walk on the track of the Hard Rock Mottos: All is One, Love All – Serve All, Take Time To Be Kind and Save the Planet.

sha.name: CHIANGMAI CELADON
sha.detail: "Chiang Mai Celadon” is a pottery product made of a qualified clay only found in Chiang Mai, the northern of Thailand. Technically, “Chiang Mai Celadon” product is made of a black clay embodied in varied design and decorated with a natural ashes liquid, then burnt at the highest temperature in an atmosphere of an incomplete combustion method which perfectly creates a beautiful crack on each product. On August 31th 2010, “Chiang Mai Celadon” is also registered as the Geographical Indication with the Department of Intellectual Property of Thailand. By this, it means that “Chiang Mai Celadon” enable to indicate the location where the product is made from. More specifically, it must be made only in Chiang Mai area.

Khun Phaen's house is near Wihan Phra Mongkhon Bophit.Si Suriyothai Park, which has a total area of 5 rai, is located within the area of the Ayutthaya liquor plant adjacent to Chedi Phra Si Suriyothai.

data.introduction: The temple was speculated to have been built right after Ayuthaya lost its independence.

data.name: Wat Khian
data.introduction: It is an ancient temple located at Mu 8, Tambon Sanchao Rong Thong, near Wat Wiset Chai Chan, which is 12 kilometers from Amphoe Mueang.

data.name: Wat Chai Mongkhon
data.introduction: The temple was built in1857, late King Rama IV period. The reason it was given the name as Chai Mongkhon because it is the victory point of boat racing while Wat Sanam Chai is the starting point of the race. Inside the temple have modern murals.

Wat Chana Songkhram
data.introduction: Situated to the north of Wat Mahathat is Wat Chana Songkhram. Its main sanctuary is a round Singhalese-style chedi. In front of the chedi exists the base of a viharn and behind the former stands an ubosot. Bases of twelve small chedis are also visible. Near Charot Withithong Road is a strange chedi having three bases, one on top of the other.

Wat Bot
data.introduction: It is located in the west of Prachin Buri on the banks of the Bang Pakong River. Inside the temple, there are 3 Buddha statues lined along the river, namely, the statue of Buddha named "Phra Siri Mongkol Nimit".

data.name: Phrakru Uthai Thamthari (Seng Sukhito) Museum
data.introduction: It is next to the King Naresuan shrine to Sa Kaeo. It is a place of prehistoric art collections and objects of neighboring countries.

data.name: King Naresuan The Great Shrine
data.introduction: It is located at the Noen Hom junction which is far from Prachin Buri to the north. Follow Highway No. 320 about 9 kilometers.

data.name: Wat Sa-nga Ngam
data.introduction: It was built on March 12, 1971 and repaired again on February 15, 2002 and also on July and October of every year.

data.name: Hat Hin Ngam
data.introduction: The beach is full of circle stones which have pretty color and are the origin of its name. There are no facilities along the beach.

data.name: Ko Yao
data.introduction: The island is located close to Wat Chon Thara Singhe. From Tak Bai District Market intersection, there is a 345-meter long wooden bridge spanning Tak Bai River to Koh Yao. The islands attraction is its eastern seaside section with a white sandy beach and cozy ambience. In addition, most of the inhabitants are Muslim fishermen who dwell in simple homes in coconut plantations.

sha.name: Paste Bangkok
sha.detail: Michelin-starred Paste Bangkok Thai Restaurant lies in the heart of Bangkok’s Ratchaprasong district adjacent to the Intercontinental Hotel, and is considered to be one of Bangkok’s best restaurants. A fusion of age-old customs and contemporary, creative twists will deliver to your plate unique, authentic, flavour-filled dishes presented in inventive and imaginative ways. Our food is intended for sharing and to be eaten with steamed jasmine rice. The following menu is a balance of textures, flavours and aromas. Our menu changes often due to market availability and produce quality.

data.name: Wat Chai Mongkhon
data.introduction: Wat Chai Mongkhon, on Phetmongkhon-Chaimongkhon Road, has a chedi that was built to house the Buddhist relics brought back from Langka by a monk named Na Issaro who was teaching Pali there in 1892.

data.name: Wat Rat Uppatham (Wat Bang Riang)
data.introduction: Situated on Khao Lan hill in Tambon Bang Riang, 11 kilometres from the Thap Put District office on Phetkasem Road, this temple contains several structures including an elaborate bell-shaped pagoda called Chedi Phutthathambanlue. The hilltop pagoda overlooks surrounding undulating mountains and hills as well as a huge Buddha image and a Guan-yin Chinese Goddess statue.

location.address: 185 Moo 4, Ban Lang, Phet Kasem Road

data.name: Wat Chaeng
data.introduction: It is located on Sunpasit road. According to the history, it was built in 1888, under the reign of King Rama V. It was the idea of Chao Ratchabutr ( Nookam), one of the four-ruler group supervising criminal law of Ubon Ratchathani in that time.

data.name: Namtok Saeng Chan (Namtok Long Ru)
data.introduction: This waterfall is only 1 kilometer from Thung Na Muang Waterfall. This is a very beautiful waterfall with a special characteristic of its own the small stream of water cascades down the face of the cliff through an opening in the rock resembling a hole. Visitors viewing the waterfall at noon when the sunlight shines through the hole will be presented with a view of the cascading water similar to that of the full moon shining onto the world. Travelling can be made from Highway No. 2134, followed by Highway No. 2112, and then turning right to the waterfall.
